  "findings": [
    "Push-in backstab connections are permitted by NEC 110.14 and device listings for 14 AWG solid copper on 15 A circuits.",
    "No visible damage, overheating, or improper installation was observed."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "No immediate corrective action required. The installation is code-compliant.",
    "Inform the homeowner that while backstab connections are allowed, many electricians prefer screw-terminal or pigtail connections for long-term reliability."
  ],
  "rationale": "The installation meets NEC 110.14 and the device listing requirements for 14 AWG solid copper on a 15 A circuit. The code permits backstab connections, so the work does not need to be redone. However, experienced electricians often avoid backstabs because they rely on a small spring contact that can loosen over time due to thermal cycling and vibration, leading to higher resistance and potential failure. Screw terminals or pigtailed connections provide a more secure, durable mechanical and electrical bond.",
  "references": [
    "NEC 110.14",
    "Device manufacturer listing (UL 498)"
  ]
}
